method: Parse production Python modules with utf-8-sig, normalize function names, and group functions whose complete AST (arguments, body, annotations, and defaults) is identical.

consolidations_completed.reason: Only AST-identical final-quality helpers with one shared contract and focused compatibility tests were consolidated.

remaining_groups.reason: Lifecycle methods belong to different runtime owners; sharing would couple unrelated model wrappers.
The renderers are independently executable report cores; a shared two-branch label helper would add coupling without removing meaningful complexity.
Each helper is private to a separate report schema; moving a trivial conversion would create a cross-domain dependency.
The final-quality private name is part of its compatibility surface while MiMo owns input ordering.
MFA local fallback must remain independently usable and must not depend on final-quality orchestration.
The implementations are structurally equal but their contracts differ: optional conversion versus segment timing extraction.
The helpers belong to glossary filtering and subtitle parsing respectively; sharing would cross ownership boundaries.
Candidate collection and local writeback guards are deliberately isolated MFA boundaries; the helper is trivial and private.
The same normalization currently serves different policy boundaries; keeping local names avoids an MFA fallback dependency inversion.
Adapter payload normalization and command-level alignment comparison are separate contracts despite matching current bodies.
The stage facade preserves command compatibility and the export command remains independently importable.
CLI stage reporting and Web status reading are separate presentation boundaries; sharing a tiny parser would couple them.
